description_long: This is a two-story brick house with a steeply pitched gable roof covered in gray tiles. The facade features a mix of rectangular windows and a prominent central entryway, with the windows arranged in an asymmetrical pattern. The house includes a small protruding section on one side, likely representing an extension or sunroom, and a chimney emerging from the roof ridge. The brickwork is uniform and reddish-brown, giving the house a traditional suburban appearance. The roof has a single black chimney and a dormer-like structure on one side, adding architectural interest. The doors and window frames are white, contrasting with the brick exterior and providing a clean, modern touch.
description: A two-story brick house with a steep gray gable roof, asymmetrical windows, and a central white entryway. The design features a chimney, dormer, and a small side extension.
description_view_0: Side view showing the steep gable roof, three windows, and a white door, with a small extension visible on the left.
description_view_1: Front view with two large garage-style doors, a central dormer, and the main roof ridge with a chimney.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with a similar gable roof, three windows, and a small extension on the right.
description_view_3: Rear view featuring a central entry door, several windows, and the dormer structure extending from the roof.
attribute_entryway_design: Central white door with no porch, flanked by windows and a dormer above, simple and functional.
